config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena tanpa perlindungan hak pribadi, kepentingan masyarakat mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk menindas orang lemah atau minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai lewat keseimbangan, bukan dengan mengorbankan kebebasan dan martabat individu. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru terbentuk ketika hak setiap orang dihormati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States we have seen how easily that idea can be used to justify abuse, discrimination, or government overreach. Public safety and the common good matter, but they should be pursued within firm limits that protect free speech, due process, privacy, and minority rights. If the collective always wins by default, then the people with the least power are usually the ones who pay the price. To me, a healthy society is one that serves the public without treating individual rights as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan warga kecil, membungkam kritik, atau mengabaikan kelompok minoritas, jadi saya tidak percaya itu bisa dijadikan prioritas mutlak. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan hukum. Kalau hak individu mudah dikorbankan, yang paling rugi biasanya justru orang yang paling lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, once you make "the public good" the higher value by default, it becomes far too easy for governments, majorities, or powerful institutions to excuse censorship, discrimination, and abuse in the name of order. That does not mean individual rights are absolute in every case, but the burden should be on society to justify any limit, not on the person to prove why their rights matter. In practice, a society is strongest when it protects the individual first and builds the common good on top of that foundation.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Di Indonesia, dalih “demi ketertiban” atau “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan warga kecil, membungkam kritik, dan mengabaikan kelompok yang tidak punya kuasa, jadi saya tidak percaya itu aman dijadikan prioritas utama. Bagi saya, kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan hukum. Kalau hak individu dikalahkan terlalu mudah, yang paling menderita biasanya justru orang yang paling lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual as a general principle. From an American perspective, I am very wary of any idea that lets "the common good" override rights by default, because that is exactly how governments and majorities justify censorship, coercion, and mistreatment of unpopular people. Public safety and social order matter, but they should be limited by strong protections for speech, due process, privacy, and minority rights. If society is healthy, it earns legitimacy by protecting individuals, not by treating them as disposable for the sake of an abstract collective.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
